description_long: This is a wall-mounted stainless steel water cooler commonly found in public urban spaces such as schools, parks, and office buildings. The unit features a rectangular basin with a gently sloped surface leading to a drain, and a single upward-angled spout for drinking water. The front panel includes two large, clearly labeled push bars for activating the water flow, one on each side for accessibility. The sides of the cooler have vented panels, likely for air circulation to the internal refrigeration unit. The overall finish is metallic with a brushed texture, and the design is utilitarian, prioritizing durability and ease of cleaning. No water bottle is visible, indicating it is a plumbed-in, direct-feed cooler.
description: A wall-mounted stainless steel water cooler with a sloped basin, push bars, and a single angled spout. It features vented side panels and a brushed metallic finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual push bars and the main basin area.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the vented panel and the push bar on the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain metallic back with no visible controls or vents.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the vented panel and the push bar on the right, as well as the spout's profile.
attribute_dispenser_spout_type: angled paddle spout with a rounded tip
attribute_control_panel_design: Two large rectangular push bars labeled 'PUSH' on the front and sides; no digital display or additional buttons visible.
